# UC-18.4.1 · Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights (NDI) Anomaly Monitoring and AIOps-Driven Fabric Diagnostics

> **Criticality:** Critical &middot; **Difficulty:** Advanced &middot; **Pillar:** Observability &middot; **Type:** Anomaly, Fault, Availability, Performance, Compliance &middot; **Wave:** Crawl &middot; **Status:** Verified

*We watch for strained links and slip-ups across your switching gear—the readings your Cisco screens already highlight—and tell repair teams what to fix before outages ripple outward.*

---

## Description

Tracks Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights anomaly forwards— **`eventType`** (**anomaly**, **compliance**, **bug**, **psirt**), **`category`** (**Resources**, **Performance**, **Connectivity**, **Operations**, **Compliance**), **`severity`** (**critical**, **major**, **warning**, **minor**, **info**), **`state`** (**active**, **acknowledged**, **cleared**), **`siteType`** (**aci**, **dcnm**, **ndfc**, **standalone-nxos**), **`objectType`** (**leaf**, **spine**, **`interface`**, **`epg`**, **`vrf`**)—including **`description`**/**`recommendation`** narratives emitted after **`NDI`** scoring plus **`NDI`** compliance evaluations (**EPG VLAN duplication**, **`policy-control-enforcement`**, **`VXLAN`** **`L3VNI`** pairing), **`NDI`** proactive **`software fault`**/**`PSIRT`** matches, endpoint (**EPG**, **`VRF`**, **endpoint mobility**, **`contract`** violations), and **`CRC`**/**link flap**/**buffer discard**/**PFC pause`** interface anomalies sourced through **`gRPC`**/**`gNMI`** telemetry aggregated inside **`ND`** clusters exporting webhook/syslog feeds tagged **`cisco:ndi:anomaly`**.

## Value

Immutable **`anomalyId`** timelines tie **`NDI`** severity and **`category`** pivots to CMDB **`siteName`**/`**siteType`** ownership so **`Connectivity`** bursts (**CRC**, **`link flap`**, **`buffer discard`**, **`PFC pause`**) escalate optics swaps faster than **`Compliance`**/`**Operations`** bursts (**CoPP**, **`logging discriminator`**, tenant cleanup noise) that belong with policy engineers—Splunkbase **7777** dashboards (**Compliance Rule Violation Trend**, **`PSIRT Match Trend`**) stay audit-ready because **`firstSeenTimestamp`**/`**lastSeenTimestamp`**/`**clearedTimestamp`** histories survive beyond **`ND`** UI retention while REST probes expose silent webhook gaps behind TLS proxies Splunk Heavy Forwarders terminate using Splunk Help HEC guidance.

## Implementation

Turn on **`NDI`** External Streaming toward Splunk HTTP Event Collector or syslog relays tagging **`cisco:ndi:anomaly`**/`**cisco:ndi:syslog`**, normalize **`camelCase`** JSON via **`FIELDALIAS`**/`**EVAL`** macros for **`anomalyId`**, **`eventType`**, **`category`**, **`severity`**, **`state`**, **`siteName`**, **`siteType`**, **`objectType`**, **`objectName`**, timestamps, wire **`MaintenanceWindow`**/`**CHG`** lookups into suppress macros, then promote Splunkbase **7777** dashboards plus alerts separating Operational backlog anomalies from leaf/spine optics faults **`Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus`** syslog corroborates.

## Detailed Implementation

### Prerequisites
- Cisco Nexus Dashboard clusters running releases 3.0–3.2 on appliances (SE-NODE-G2, **ND**A-K9, NDA1100) or virtual OVA/KVM/Hyper-V typically size three master nodes plus workers per Cisco worksheets before scaling **NDI** Sites from **APIC**, **DCNM**/**NDFC**, or standalone **NX-OS** fabrics documented inside Cisco Nexus Dashboard clustering guidance.
- Nexus Dashboard Insights builds 6.4–6.6 upgrade independently from the platform train—coordinate with **Nexus Dashboard Orchestrator** or **Nexus Dashboard Fabric Controller** siblings and **Azure NDC** attachments that can precede hybrid rollouts.
- Enable **NDI Settings** → System Settings → External Streaming → Splunk targeting **HTTP Event Collector** endpoints on 443/tcp with sourcetype=cisco:ndi:anomaly; syslog (UDP/TCP/TLS) bridges use sourcetype=cisco:ndi:syslog when HTTPS policies block outbound automation and pair with **Splunk Connect for Syslog** or Heavy Forwarders.
- Deploy **cisco_dc_networking_app_for_splunk** (**Splunkbase 7777**) for dashboard packs (Anomaly Volume by Severity, Top-N Sites, Compliance Rule Violation Trend, **PSIRT** Match Trend) and add **Splunk_TA_cisco-aci** (4022) plus **Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus** (6876) when APIC/NX-OS syslog must contextualize leaf/spine **gRPC/gNMI**-driven stories inside NDI anomalies.
- **LDAP**/**SAML** directories (LDAP, Active Directory, **RADIUS**, **TACACS+**, **OneLogin**, **Okta**, **Microsoft Entra ID**) should map **Site Manager** versus **Approver** privileges to exporter scope; carry **siteName** tenancy into **Splunk lookups** so centralized indexes avoid unauthorized Sites.

### Step 1 — Configure anomaly exports from Nexus Dashboard Insights
(1) HEC webhook: paste https://<splunk-hec>/services/collector/event plus collector tokens inside NDI External Streaming—curl Authorization: Splunk <token> probes validate parity before sustained **POST** traffic; never stash personal passwords inside automation secrets beside **Streaming Settings**.
(2) Syslog relay: send events to Splunk Connect for Syslog or Heavy Forwarders—preserve JSON under **FAC/PRI** via props **LINE_BREAKER** and sourcetype=cisco:ndi:syslog—**KV_MODE=json** speeds **anomalyId** extraction when **reverse proxies** prepend cluster metadata headers.
(3) REST augmentation: optional scripted **GET** https://<nd-cluster>/api/v1/ anomaly endpoints from **Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights API Reference**—honor backoff on HTTP 429/503 surfaced in diagnostics bundles—prefer **webhook-first** postures whenever TLS POST from worker pods stays allowed.
(4) Index routing: align indexes.conf with Splunkbase 7777 (commonly index=cisco_dc or network_operations) and stamp siteType facets (aci, dcnm, ndfc, standalone-nxos) before alerting on Resources versus Performance versus Connectivity versus Operations versus Compliance facets.

### Step 2 — SPL authoring guidance for anomaly correlation
Execute the SPL below once anomalyId JSON lands in the index—**FIELDALIAS** hedges camelCase drift, rename macros collapse **eventType** aliases, and spath unwraps **TLS-proxy** payloads between Nexus Dashboard exporters and Heavy Forwarders terminating HTTP Event Collector listeners described in ****Splunk Help** TLS** articles.

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndi:anomaly" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:syslog") earliest=-24h
| eval **anomaly_key**=trim(coalesce(anomalyId, anomaly_id, guid))
| eval evt_type=lower(trim(coalesce(eventType, event_type)))
| eval cat_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(category, Category)))
| eval sev_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(severity, Severity)))
| eval obj_name=trim(coalesce(objectName, object_name))
| eval **site_label**=trim(coalesce(siteName, site_name, fabric_site))
| eval state_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(state, anomaly_state)))
| eval ts_first=strptime(trim(coalesce(**firstSeenTimestamp**, first_seen)), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
| eval ts_last=strptime(trim(coalesce(**lastSeenTimestamp**, last_seen)), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
| where isnull(evt_type) OR evt_type IN ("anomaly","compliance","bug","psirt")
| where state_lc IN ("active","acknowledged") OR state_lc="" OR isnull(state_lc)
| eval hotspot=case(
    cat_lc="resources", "capacity_contract",
    cat_lc="performance", "qos_buffer",
    cat_lc="connectivity", "crc_link_flap",
    cat_lc="operations", "process_health",
    cat_lc="compliance", "policy_rules",
    match(lower(coalesce(description,"")), "(?i)pfc"), "microburst_pause",
    match(lower(coalesce(description,"")), "(?i)crc"), "physical_crc",
    match(lower(coalesce(description,"")), "(?i)contract"), "endpoint_contract",
    1=1, "general_ndi")
| stats dc(anomaly_key) as anomaly_instances,
        dc(site_label) as sites_impacted,
        values(obj_name) as objects,
        max(ts_last) as recent_evidence,
        min(ts_first) as earliest_evidence,
        latest(description) as narrative,
        latest(recommendation) as recommended_fix
    by site_label, cat_lc, sev_lc, evt_type, hotspot
| eval severity_rank=case(sev_lc=="critical",1,sev_lc=="major",2,sev_lc=="warning",3,sev_lc=="minor",4,5)
| sort severity_rank, -anomaly_instances
| head 250
```

Field rationale bullets:
- anomaly_key dedupes webhook retries that replay identical anomalyId values—stats reflect true incident counts versus HTTP resend noise visible in splunkd **HttpInputProc** logs.
- cat_lc/hotspot mirrors NDI taxonomy—Connectivity rows flag **CRC**s, link flaps, buffers, **PFC** pauses mapped to **Network_Traffic** stories; Compliance rows cite VLAN duplication, policy-control posture, **VXLAN**/**L3VNI** pairings, or **CoPP**/logging rules from Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights Release 6.4 and Splunkbase 7777 bundles.
- sev_lc buckets align to Alerts semantics operations teams macro-map—evt_type separates behavioral anomalies from compliance bugs versus proactive PSIRT **Software Fault** matches.
- site_label enrichment from **ServiceNow** CMDB macros lets **MaintenanceWindow** suppressions distinguish deliberate migrations from stealth outages when duplicate friendly site labels appear mid-merge.

Correlation cadence: drive paging macros with where sev_lc IN ("critical","major"); keep warning/minor/info on hygiene dashboards tied to Nexus Dashboard state active/acknowledged/cleared—suppress using MaintenanceWindow identifiers from ServiceNow so anomaly_key/site_label CMDB lookups respect tenancy boundaries without masking overlapping bridges during disciplined migrations **Splunk **SOC**** reviewers rehearse quarterly.

### Step 3 — Validation rituals beside Nexus Dashboard Insights consoles
(1) NDI Insights → Anomalies filters must match Splunk site_label/siteName pairs—adjust trim macros—and compare objectType/objectName for leaf/spine/interface/epg/vrf stories that cite gNMI telemetry in **Cisco Nexus Dashboard Insights User Guide Release 6.4**.
(2) bug/psirt eventType lines get compared to **show version** output via Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus syslog—check firstSeenTimestamp/lastSeenTimestamp/**clearedTimestamp** versus Site Manager/Approver/**Power User** acknowledgements documented under Nexus Dashboard **RBAC** and **Nexus Dashboard Documentation Hub** SAML guidance (OneLogin, Okta, Microsoft Entra ID).
(3) Cleared anomalies that stay active usually mean syslog truncation dropped clearedTimestamp bits—retune LINE_BREAKER and correlate splunkd HttpInputProc with exporter jitter in Streaming Settings bundles before blaming fabrics.
(4) **REST parity** checks: curl documented /api/v1/ **UUID** lists to catch silent webhook gaps whenever exporter backoff hides POST failures even though dashboards look green.

### Step 4 — Operationalise Cisco DC Networking Application workspaces
(1) Pin Splunkbase 7777 dashboards to war-room displays using **site_kind_lc** to separate aci, dcnm, ndfc, and standalone-nxos behaviours.
(2) Copy NDI recommendation text into tickets verbatim so Cisco TAC engagements keep engineering language.
(3) Throttle alerts with MaintenanceWindow macros so Operations spikes (CoPP, logging discriminators) during scripted cleanups do not page on-call during approved changes.
(4) Export weekly **CSV** evidence for **PCI**/**SOX** reviews when Compliance anomalies must stay separate from Connectivity CRC noise in audit binders.
(5) Document overlaps between Nexus Dashboard delegated roles (Site Manager/Approver/Power User) and analytics RBAC guarding HTTP Event Collector secrets rotated with collector tokens quarterly.

### Step 5 — Troubleshooting patterns
- **HEC 401/403 loops** — rotate Streaming Settings tokens against **tokens.conf**—curl probes must mirror automation secrets quarterly.
- **Syslog JSON truncation** — LINE_BREAKER drift when FAC/PRI or gzip chunking hides JSON tails—spath helps when KV_MODE=json misses fields.
- **CamelCase drift** — FIELDALIAS/rename macros align eventType and siteName after NDI upgrades before stats dedup anomaly_key.
- **Duplicate anomalyId bursts** — webhook timeouts resend payloads—dedup anomaly_key before KPI rollups and compare HttpInputProc timing to exporter logs in diagnostics bundles.
- **Cleared anomalies lag** — partial syslog frames miss clearedTimestamp—use **summary indexing** for **MTTA**/**MTTR**, not destructive deletes in production.
- **REST 429/503 storms** — backoff scripts for /api/v1/ GET collectors—prefer webhooks whenever policy allows sustained TLS POST from worker pods.
- **TLS interception** — enterprise PKI anchors must exist on Nexus Dashboard hosts and Splunk Heavy Forwarder cacert.pem bundles deployed via **deployment server**.
- **siteName collisions** — CMDB lookups reconcile duplicate friendly names typed during onboarding so EPG/contract anomalies map to correct fabric scope.
- **Tenant teardown compliance floods** — asynchronous ACI deletes temporarily break VLAN uniqueness—suppress until CMDB reflects deletions scheduled in ServiceNow.
- **Patch Tuesday PSIRT waves** — bug-database refreshes inflate Software Fault rows—delay paging until lastSeenTimestamp deltas flatten week over week.
- **ND worker saturation** — **kubectl**/**docker stats** differentiate CPU/RAM starvation on the cluster from fabric CRC spikes that need optics—correlate NX-OS telemetry replays inside change windows.

## SPL

```spl
(index=cisco_dc OR index=cisco_network OR index=network_operations)
(sourcetype="cisco:ndi:anomaly" OR sourcetype="cisco:ndi:syslog") earliest=-24h
| eval anomaly_key=trim(coalesce(anomalyId, anomaly_id, guid))
| eval evt_type=lower(trim(coalesce(eventType, event_type)))
| eval cat_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(category, Category)))
| eval sev_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(severity, Severity)))
| eval obj_name=trim(coalesce(objectName, object_name))
| eval site_label=trim(coalesce(siteName, site_name, fabric_site))
| eval state_lc=lower(trim(coalesce(state, anomaly_state)))
| eval ts_first=strptime(trim(coalesce(firstSeenTimestamp, first_seen)), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
| eval ts_last=strptime(trim(coalesce(lastSeenTimestamp, last_seen)), "%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%S.%3NZ")
| where isnull(evt_type) OR evt_type IN ("anomaly","compliance","bug","psirt")
| where state_lc IN ("active","acknowledged") OR state_lc="" OR isnull(state_lc)
| eval hotspot=case(
    cat_lc="resources", "capacity_contract",
    cat_lc="performance", "qos_buffer",
    cat_lc="connectivity", "crc_link_flap",
    cat_lc="operations", "process_health",
    cat_lc="compliance", "policy_rules",
    match(lower(coalesce(description,"")), "(?i)pfc"), "microburst_pause",
    match(lower(coalesce(description,"")), "(?i)crc"), "physical_crc",
    match(lower(coalesce(description,"")), "(?i)contract"), "endpoint_contract",
    1=1, "general_ndi")
| stats dc(anomaly_key) as anomaly_instances,
        dc(site_label) as sites_impacted,
        values(obj_name) as objects,
        max(ts_last) as recent_evidence,
        min(ts_first) as earliest_evidence,
        latest(description) as narrative,
        latest(recommendation) as recommended_fix
    by site_label, cat_lc, sev_lc, evt_type, hotspot
| eval severity_rank=case(sev_lc=="critical",1,sev_lc=="major",2,sev_lc=="warning",3,sev_lc=="minor",4,5)
| sort severity_rank, -anomaly_instances
| head 250
```

## Visualization

(1) Splunkbase **7777** — **Anomaly Volume by Severity** with **`sev_lc`** overlays; (2) **7777** — **Anomaly Volume by Category** pivoting **`cat_lc`**/**`hotspot`**; (3) **7777** — **Top-N Sites with Most Anomalies** keyed on **`site_label`**; (4) **7777** — **Top-N Endpoint Mobility Anomalies** highlighting **`objectType`** **`epg`**/**`vrf`** tuples; (5) **7777** — **Compliance Rule Violation Trend** filtered **`eventType=compliance`**; (6) **7777** — **PSIRT Match Trend** for **`eventType`** **`bug`**/**`psirt`** narratives paired with **`Splunk_TA_cisco-nexus`** syslog snapshots.

## Known False Positives

Planned **ACI tenant teardown** sequences briefly break VLAN uniqueness checks until asynchronous deletes finish—**NDI Compliance** anomalies surge even when ServiceNow tickets already cover the work. **Patch Tuesday** Cisco PSIRT metadata refreshes inside **NDI** bug libraries can spike **Software Fault** counts for NX-OS trains that still pass your last quarterly sign-off—delay paging until **lastSeenTimestamp** deltas flatten versus the prior week. **Microburst contention** can raise **Performance** anomalies with **PFC pause** signatures that self-clear when queues drain—tie paging to sustained **critical** streaks beyond the settling window Cisco documents. **Nexus Dashboard cluster** members that restart can backlog **gNMI** collectors so **Operations** anomalies look like fabric faults until **kubectl** or **docker stats** prove the node—not the spine—was starving. **Fresh Site** baselines often generate **info/minor** posture rows while **NDI** learns daily patterns—hold SLAs until the burn-in timers in **Nexus Dashboard Insights** documentation expire. **NX-OS configuration replay** storms can overrun **streaming telemetry** buffers and briefly look like **Connectivity** gaps—confirm with **show telemetry** on the switch before scheduling optic replacements unrelated to queue pressure.
